user_prompt: simply start LEDC with ESP-IDF calls, on GPIO14, use the lowest possible resolution to achieve 50% duty cycle, frequency should be 8192000
agent_reply: It now starts ESP-IDF LEDC on GPIO14 at `8192000` Hz using `LEDC_TIMER_1_BIT`, with duty `1`, which gives the requested 50% duty cycle at the lowest useful resolution. It prints the LEDC timer/channel setup result and then idles so the waveform stays active.
Validation: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passes. I restored `src/version.c` afterward so the build-generated version change is not left in the diff.

user_prompt: do a build, fix the linker error
agent_reply: Build reproduced the linker error and it’s fixed now.

The issue was that `AudioManager.h` declared public `AudioManager::configure_i2s_shared()`, but `AudioManager.cpp` only defined a same-named function inside an anonymous namespace, so the public symbol was never emitted. I renamed the hidden implementation to `configure_i2s_shared_impl()` and added the public wrapper in [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:804).
Validation: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` now succeeds. I restored `src/version.c` to its pre-build state; it was already dirty when I started, so I left that existing change alone.

user_prompt: implement public `ExtCodec::start_ledc_mclk`, basically a copy of `test_ledcpwm`, move `kOutputGpio` to `pins.h` as `kLedcMclkGpio`
agent_reply: It’s public in [ExtCodec.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.h:39), implemented in [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:200), and uses LEDC high-speed mode at `8192000 Hz`, `LEDC_TIMER_1_BIT`, duty `1`, on the new [kLedcMclkGpio](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:22).
I also updated `test_ledcpwm.cpp` to use `kLedcMclkGpio` instead of its local `kOutputGpio`.
Validation: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passes. I left `src/version.c` as it was before this task since it was already dirty from generated-version churn.
